title: Composite view as a first-class tool
oneline: gen:coloring-composite built, byte-identical to ad hoc lib use, gate-override demo works
summary: Implemented gen-coloring-composite.mjs: an offline CLI writing the final light+night canvas renders of any page/category to .coloring-samples/composite/, for shipped assets, fill-src raws, and scratch candidates. Night side calls lib/night-composite.mjs verbatim — sha256-identical to the historical ad hoc node -e usage on all three cases tried. Demonstrated the gate-override workflow on train-wide: shipped raw scores lineWhite 75 and candidate sample-1 scores 102 (both hard gate fails, bar 150) yet both composites render perfect white chalk lines. Wired per ADR-0019 (root script + scripts-info + local alias) with README/pipeline.md mentions.
tried: CLI with drift-audit-style page/category selectors and --source shipped|raw|samples
Night composite via verbatim compositeNight() for lined fills; screen-only variant for already-punched shipped fills
Light composite (punch-to-paper + pen multiply) mirroring the contact-sheet client
sha256 byte-compare of tool output vs ad hoc node-e style compositeNight calls
Offline replica of the scoreLineColor gate to score the scratch candidates for the worked example
npm wiring: gen:coloring-composite + scripts-info entry + tools/asset-gen local alias, eslint/prettier clean
worked: Byte-identical night composites to the ad hoc lib usage (train-wide raw, cat-tall raw, train-wide sample-1 candidate)
All three sources on the try-on pages: shipped, raw, and the real train-wide candidates already sitting in .coloring-samples-dark/
Gate-override demo: lineWhite 75/102 gate-fails whose composites render perfectly (the chalk owns the lines)
Shipped vs raw composite pixel-diff confirms the no-repunch shipped path (edge-only noise, meanAbs <1.5)
limitations: Light-candidate path implemented but untested against real files (no light samples existed in scratch; empty-dir skip verified)
compositeLight grayscales the pen before multiply (matching the lib's chalk handling); canvas multiplies per-channel RGB — nil for the grayscale line art
Outputs are PNG (~1-2 MB each) in gitignored scratch; a --webp flag would be a cheap follow-up
lib/night-composite.mjs deliberately untouched; the shipped-fill screen variant lives in the CLI
